name: Vandorallen Trebatius
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears middle-class clothing consisting of a quilted doublet, tan linens, and a pair of russet felt shoes - respectable attire that suggests someone trying to maintain appearances despite financial troubles.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: financially struggling, social, displaced, persistent, militiaman
Role: Vandorallen Trebatius is a knight and apparently a member of Skingrad's local militia. He is the current owner of Rosethorn Hall, though he's delinquent on his rent and faces eviction if someone purchases the property from the Count. Despite his knightly status, his financial situation is precarious enough that he cannot keep up with his obligations.
Home: Rosethorn Hall in Skingrad - a stately manor he currently occupies but does not truly own, having fallen behind on rent. After eviction, he relocates to the Two Sisters Lodge, though he's unable to sleep there properly due to his circumstances.
Daily Routine (Before Eviction): Vandorallen wakes at 8am on the top floor of Rosethorn Hall. He spends time at the Guard House from 8am to 10am, reflecting his militia membership. At 10am he goes to the Two Sisters Lodge for a two-hour breakfast. At midday he walks up to Castle Skingrad and spends six hours wandering the Main Hall until 6pm. From 6pm to 9pm he wanders the ground floor of his home at Rosethorn Hall. At 9pm he heads to the West Weald Inn for a three-hour supper, returning home at midnight to sleep for eight hours.
Daily Routine (After Eviction): His schedule changes significantly after losing Rosethorn Hall. He still wakes at 8am but now at the Two Sisters Lodge, where he cannot properly sleep. He maintains his 8am-10am time at the Guard House, has breakfast at the Two Sisters Lodge from 10am-noon, spends midday to 6pm at Castle Skingrad's Main Hall as before, but now spends 6pm-9pm at the Guard House (three hours instead of relaxing at home). He still has supper at the West Weald Inn from 9pm-midnight before returning to the Two Sisters Lodge where he cannot rest properly.
Militia Connection: As an apparent member of Skingrad's local militia, Vandorallen spends his free time at the Guard House. This suggests he maintains some connection to civic duty and defense despite his financial struggles, or perhaps he seeks purpose and structure there as his personal life falls apart.
Magical Abilities: Vandorallen possesses a leveled set of knight spells, indicating formal training in both martial and magical arts befitting his knightly status.
Personality - The Displaced Knight: Vandorallen represents a fall from grace - a knight who cannot afford to maintain his stately manor despite his title and status. He continues going through the motions of his life, eating at proper establishments, visiting the castle, maintaining his militia duties, but the foundation is crumbling beneath him. His delinquency on rent suggests either mismanagement, declining fortunes, or perhaps expenses that exceed his income. After eviction, he maintains his social routines and militia involvement, but the loss of his ability to sleep properly at the Two Sisters Lodge symbolizes his displacement - a man without a true home, going through familiar patterns but unable to find rest. He appears to have no close ties warning him of the impending eviction or helping him avoid it, suggesting either isolation or exhausted goodwill.
The Eviction: Shum gro-Yarug handles Vandorallen's removal with bureaucratic efficiency, assuring the new owner that the knight will be evicted and all his belongings thrown out. The orc expresses confidence that Vandorallen will "find another home easily," though this proves somewhat ironic given his subsequent inability to even sleep properly at the lodge. The eviction is treated as a mere business transaction, with no apparent concern for the displaced knight's welfare.
author: Bethesda
